content: Oqto mini-app SDK lives in frontend/mini-apps/: theming/ (base24 scheme -> semantic CSS var mapping via mapSchemeToTokens+applyScheme, oqto-dark/light pinned schemes), sdk/ (OqtoHost contract + defineOqtoApp + createMockHost), shell/ (OqtoAppShell applies scheme + injects host), workbench/ (2nd Vite entry at /workbench.html). Apps run standalone with a mock host, no backend. base24 swaps reskin the whole app. Integration phase (real host, app-registry wiring, a2ui removal) is trx oqto-trdk.5.

Oqto mini-app SDK now has 2 apps in frontend/mini-apps/: photo-editor (PixiJS ColorMatrixFilter adjustments+crop) and photo-grid (resizable collage: draggable row/col dividers via fr units, per-tile cover-crop with object-position pan, batch pool via host.files.pickMultiple, canvas compositor export in lib/cover.ts). OqtoHost gained pickMultiple. Workbench shows a launcher when >1 app. DOM/CSS used for grid (cover-fit maps to auto-crop).

oqto-sandbox build_bwrap_args_for_user seeds the sandbox PATH with canonical system bin dirs (/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin) plus user toolchain dirs (~/.cargo/bin, ~/go/bin, ~/.local/bin, ~/.bun/bin, ~/.npm-global/bin), appended to the inherited PATH if missing+existing. This guarantees bare command resolution (sed, gitleaks) inside the sandbox even when launched from a sparse-PATH context (systemd/cron). It bind-mounts /usr,/bin,/sbin so absolute paths always work; PATH seeding is what makes bare names work too.
